perturbation.original_text: Subject to all other terms and conditions set forth herein, as of the date of this agreement, LEADER maintain the software for an additional five years.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to all other terms and conditions set forth herein, as of the date of this agreement, LEADER shall maintain the software for an additional five years, contingent upon EZJR's continued satisfaction with LEADER's performance, as determined solely by EZJR.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conditionality to LEADER's obligation to maintain the software. While section 1 states LEADER 'shall maintain', the added clause allows EZJR to unilaterally decide if LEADER's performance is satisfactory, potentially voiding LEADER's obligation. This contradicts the original unconditional commitment to maintain the software for five years. This creates a conflict with Section 5, ENTIRE AGREEMENT, that states 'This Agreement contains the entire agreement among the parties, and supersedes all prior agreements, representations and understandings of the parties, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.' The introduced conditionality would not be superseding other agreements, representations, and understandings, and would be subject to EZJR's opinion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement among the parties, and supersedes all prior agreements, representations and understandings of the parties, relating to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that LEADER 'maintain the software for an additional five years'. The changed text introduces a contingency based on EZJR's satisfaction, effectively giving EZJR the power to void LEADER's obligation. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states that the agreement 'contains the entire agreement among the parties, and supersedes all prior agreements, representations and understandings'. By adding a condition based on EZJR's satisfaction, the changed text introduces an element of subjectivity and potential dispute that is not fully captured in the 'entire agreement', therefore it is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement has been duly authorized and approved by the Board of Directors of LEADER, and this Agreement constitutes a valid and binding Agreement of LEADER in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: The execution, delivery, and performance of this Agreement has been duly authorized and approved by a majority of the Board of Directors of LEADER, however, this Agreement's enforceability is subject to review and potential revocation by a newly elected board within the first 60 days. This Agreement constitutes what is believed to be a valid and binding Agreement of LEADER in accordance with its terms, pending the aforementioned review.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty regarding the validity of the agreement. Section 3.B represents that the agreement is 'valid and binding'. The additional clause now makes the agreement's validity conditional and subject to change. This contradicts with Section 8, SUCCESSORS AND ASSIGNS, in the way that 'This Agreement will be binding on, and will inure to the benefit of, the parties and their respective successors and assigns, and shall not confer any rights or remedies on any other Persons.' The agreement is being introduced as not binding and will cause issues if the board is being reviewed and potentially revoked.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will be binding on, and will inure to the benefit of, the parties and their respective successors and assigns, and shall not confer any rights or remedies on any other Persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition that the agreement's enforceability is subject to review and potential revocation, which directly contradicts the original binding nature stated in the contradicted text: 'This Agreement will be binding on, and will inure to the benefit of...'. The original text does not have a review and potential revocation, but the change does and therefore directly contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted hereunder will be in writing and will be deemed to have been duly given when delivered by hand, by overnight courier, or fax, or two days after being mailed by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, with postage prepaid.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted hereunder will be in writing and will be deemed to have been duly given when delivered by hand or by email with confirmation receipt, or two days after being mailed by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, with postage prepaid. However, critical legal notices must be delivered exclusively by carrier pigeon to be considered valid.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces contradictory instructions for delivering 'critical legal notices'. While standard notices can use conventional methods like mail and email, critical legal notices must be delivered by carrier pigeon, which is impractical and creates an inconsistency. This makes the method of notice unclear and creates a contradiction with the start of Section 12 that states 'All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted hereunder will be in writing'. Pigeon carrier is not writing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices, requests, demands, and other communications required or permitted hereunder will be in writing and will be deemed to have been duly given when delivered by hand or by email with confirmation receipt, or two days after being mailed by certified or registered mail, return receipt requested, with postage prepaid. However, critical legal notices must be delivered exclusively by carrier pigeon to be considered valid.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states that all notices will be in writing. The changed text introduces an exception for 'critical legal notices', which must be delivered by carrier pigeon, which is not in writing. This directly contradicts the initial statement that 'all notices... will be in writing', creating a clear and significant inconsistency within the document regarding acceptable methods of delivering notices, and also contradicting the contradicted text.
